description:
## Problem
Three different concepts all called 'validate': (1) agent /validate slash command (workflow stage 3), (2) forge-validate CLI (bin/forge-validate.js), (3) bun run check / validate.sh (local quality gate). This causes confusion about which to use when.

## Fixes Required
1. Three concepts share the name 'validate' with no disambiguation

## Improvements
- UX: Short doc explaining: 'for prerequisites use X; for local CI-like gate use Y; in the agent use Z'
- Technical: Optional aliases (bun run validate → check) or rename one path to reduce collision
title: Epic: Naming overload — three different 'validate' concepts

description:
## Problem
Users must manually specify which agent to set up. The CLI should detect which agent is running based on env vars and directory presence.

## Signals
- CLAUDE_CODE env var or .claude/ exists → Claude Code
- .cursor/ exists or CURSOR_* env vars → Cursor
- .cline/ exists → Cline
- .codex/ exists → Codex
- None → ask (interactive) or default to claude (--auto)

## Use Case
npx forge setup --auto from inside Claude Code → sets up only Claude. Zero prompts.
title: Auto-detect current AI agent from environment signals

description:
## Description
Dolt/Beads remains a recurring source of setup, sync, push, and worktree friction. Forge should prioritize a TypeScript API-friendly Kernel authority path and keep Dolt/Beads as projection/import-export compatibility rather than core runtime state.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#7-dolt-remains-a-recurring-operational-pain-point`

## Scope
- Identify current command paths that still require Dolt for normal Forge workflow operations.
- Define the TS API-friendly local Kernel interface needed to replace Dolt in the hot path.
- Cover ready/show/list/claim/update/comment/close/stage/run/projection-status command surfaces.
- Preserve Beads/Dolt import/export/projection fidelity until migration gates pass.
- Add release gates that distinguish Dolt compatibility from Dolt authority.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A documented command/path inventory separates hot-path Dolt usage from compatibility/projection usage.
- Kernel local authority API requirements are ready for implementation PRs.
- Normal Forge workflow commands do not shell out to `bd`, read `.beads/issues.jsonl`, treat Beads/Dolt sync as authority, or require Dolt installation except inside import/export/projection adapters.
- Migration tests prove no issue/state/projection data is silently lost.
- Gates include unsupported Beads field preservation, real Dolt-backed ready-queue parity fixtures, import/export dry-run reports, rollback docs, echo-loop prevention, projection quarantine, and proof projection failures never override Kernel authority.
- Release notes cannot claim Dolt is retired until parity, rollback, and projection gates pass.
title: Retire Dolt from Forge hot path behind TypeScript Kernel API

description:
## Description
Pre-merge behavior should not become a universal standalone workflow stage. It should be an embedded gate/checkpoint inside existing task-type-specific processes.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#6-pre-merge-should-not-become-a-universal-standalone-stage`

## Scope
- Audit workflow taxonomy, command templates, generated prompts, and tests for pre-merge stage assumptions.
- Define pre-merge as a gate/checkpoint owned by task classification, not a top-level mandatory stage.
- Update docs and command contracts so docs-only, feature, hotfix, refactor, and architecture tasks can require different gates.
- Preserve branch protection and CI checks as merge requirements without creating stage drift.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow stage model does not add universal `premerge` as a required stage for every task.
- Task-type profiles can embed pre-merge checks where appropriate.
- Generated instructions and tests use consistent terminology: stages vs gates/checkpoints.
- Existing workflow commands remain backward compatible where possible.

## Description
Design the Project Knowledge Layer MVP using a verbatim-first read model inspired by MemPalace.

## Scope
- Index source artifacts without summarizing them away.
- Include plan.md, legacy design.md, tasks.md, decisions.md, evidence.md, issue bodies, comments, stage runs, validation logs.
- Store metadata for issue, release, sprint, stage, actor/session, source path, and event id.
- Start with FTS5/read model; vectors can be a later backend.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reference design exists for the index schema and rebuild behavior.
- Summaries/facts are defined as derived proposals with source refs.
- Search can be scoped before global/deep retrieval.

## Description
The migration from `design.md` to `plan.md` is incomplete across docs, skills, command templates, tests, and generated agent guidance. This creates inconsistent instructions and weakens Project Knowledge indexing.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#3-designmd-to-planmd-migration-is-incomplete`

## Scope
- Audit repository references to `design.md`, `plan.md`, work-item planning, design docs, and command contracts.
- Seed the audit with known stale/default-work-item surfaces: `AGENTS.md`, `.claude/commands/plan.md`, `.claude/commands/dev.md`, `.claude/rules/workflow.md`, `lib/core/runtime-graph.js`, and `test/structural/command-contracts.test.js`.
- Replace default work-item planning references with `plan.md`.
- Preserve `design.md` support for true durable architecture/product designs and legacy reads.
- Update drift guards and tests to prevent the old default from returning.
- Ensure KnowledgeStore and orient/recap source classification distinguish `work_plan`, `legacy_work_design`, `architecture_design`, `task_list`, `decision_log`, `evidence`, `issue_body`, `comment`, `stage_run`, `generated_harness`, `runtime_projection`, `derived_summary`, and `proposal`.
- Define proof artifact classification for `evidence.md`, `validation.md`, `validation-notes.md`, stage-run logs, and command evidence.
- Define the minimum evidence artifact contract for Project Knowledge ingestion: issue/work item id, stage/task id, command/run id, command string or tool name, exit code/verdict, timestamp, actor/session, stdout/stderr or log path, source file refs, commit SHA/revision/hash, redaction state, provenance/source type, and links back to the relevant `tasks.md` and `decisions.md` entries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Search results show no command/template/skill teaching `design.md` as the default work-item planning output.
- Legacy `design.md` inputs are still read where backward compatibility is required.
- `forge orient` and `forge recap` prefer `plan.md`, label legacy `design.md` correctly, surface missing `tasks.md`/`decisions.md`/evidence, and expose JSON source types.
- Evidence artifacts missing the minimum contract are surfaced as incomplete evidence/proposals rather than accepted proof.
- Tests cover command contract alignment, KnowledgeStore source classification, and stale reference regression fixtures.
- Docs clearly state `plan.md` is for per-work-item intent/research/approach while `design.md` is reserved for durable design.
title: Complete design.md to plan.md migration across commands skills and docs

## Description
Worktrees can miss Lefthook/lint setup or have stale hook paths, so local validation may not actually run where agents are working.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#4-hooks-and-lint-checks-are-not-reliably-installed-in-worktrees`

## Scope
- Extend `forge hooks doctor --json` to inspect linked worktrees, git common-dir hooks, `core.hooksPath`, Lefthook install state, lint command availability, and agent adapter status.
- Define exact Git path checks: main worktree vs linked worktree, `git rev-parse --git-common-dir`, `git rev-parse --git-path hooks`, and relative/absolute `core.hooksPath` normalization.
- Define lint discovery order: Forge workflow config, package-manager scripts, repo-local binaries, and explicit fallback command.
- Implement safe `forge hooks install/sync` repair behavior for worktree-local adapters.
- Support `--json` and `--dry-run` for doctor/install/sync.
- Surface hook/lint installation status in `forge validate` and `forge push` before claiming gates are active.
- Keep Lefthook as an adapter; Forge policy engine remains authority.
- Do not write global/private agent profiles without explicit authorization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Doctor detects missing, stale, disabled, or misdirected worktree hook/lint setup.
- Repair commands are explicit, narrow, and safe for Windows/MSYS and POSIX worktrees.
- Tests cover normal checkout, linked worktree, missing Lefthook, stale `core.hooksPath`, and absent lint command.
- Protected workflow/hook files are only changed through Forge-owned protected-state-aware surfaces.
- `forge push` cannot silently claim local gate coverage when worktree hook/lint setup is absent.
title: Ensure hook and lint installation is reliable in linked worktrees

## Description
New worktrees, especially from older Forge versions, may not have usable Forge state. This blocks issue claiming, validation, hook enforcement, and workflow continuation.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#5-forge-state-can-be-unconfigured-in-new-worktrees`

## Scope
- Add a worktree state doctor for `.forge`, `.beads`/projection state, Kernel DB/bootstrap state, git common-dir mapping, adoption profile, and hooks.
- Key the canonical Kernel DB/bootstrap location by git common-dir and treat `.beads` as projection/import state, not authority.
- Determine whether the issue still exists in the current release branch with fixtures for old-version and current-version worktrees.
- Provide repair commands that initialize missing state without overwriting authority/projection data.
- Include backup/quarantine behavior for partial or old worktree state and embed hook doctor status as a sub-result instead of duplicating checks inconsistently.
- Ensure worktree state uses the intended local authority path and does not depend on Dolt as hot-path authority.
- Route repair writes through protected-state-aware Forge APIs and declared surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Doctor reports configured/unconfigured/partial state with machine-readable JSON.
- Repeated doctor/repair is idempotent and becomes no-op after first success.
- Fresh current-version worktrees pass without manual stashing or ad hoc setup.
- Old-version worktree fixtures either self-heal or produce exact repair steps.
- Existing authority/projection data is never overwritten; partial state is backed up or quarantined with evidence.
- Regression tests cover Windows/MSYS paths and git common-dir mapping.
title: Add Forge state doctor and bootstrap repair for new worktrees

description:
## Description
Forge lifecycle commands can leave generated docs/harness/runtime files dirty during push, review, verify, merge, or post-merge cleanup. This forces agents to stash generated files just to close the workflow.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#1-generated-forge-docsharness-files-reappear-during-lifecycle-commands`

## Scope
- Audit `forge status`, `forge validate`, `forge push`, `forge ship`, review helpers, setup/update paths, and post-merge cleanup for generated file writes.
- Identify files that are durable planning artifacts vs generated harness outputs vs runtime/projection state.
- Define a generated-state manifest/contract for each generated surface: owner command/API, protected-state category, source inputs, content hash/version, line-ending policy, timestamp policy, and whether it is generated projection, runtime/projection state, or durable artifact.
- Make no-op regeneration content-addressed/idempotent so unchanged files do not become dirty.
- Route intentional generated writes through owning Forge surfaces with clear evidence/repair hints.
- Add diagnostics that explain whether dirty generated files are expected, stale, or unsafe.
- Add clean-checkout + linked-worktree lifecycle smoke coverage for push, review, verify, merge, and post-merge cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reproducing a push/review/verify/merge lifecycle on a clean checkout does not leave generated Forge docs/harness files dirty when no semantic content changed.
- If generated files are stale, Forge prints a single safe command to update them and explains the owning surface.
- Tests cover pre/post `git status --porcelain`, CRLF/line-ending stability, timestamp-free generation, backup snapshot stability, repeated command idempotency, and protected-state interaction.
- Generated-file writers use atomic compare-before-write behavior and do not rewrite identical content.
- Normal repair guidance never says to stash generated files as the expected workflow path.
- Agents no longer need to stash generated files to close a normal workflow.
title: Stop generated Forge docs and harness churn during lifecycle commands

description:
## Description
Fresh Forge adoption does not consistently teach or scaffold the current work-folder planning structure. New projects should guide agents toward `docs/work/<date>-<slug>/plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `decisions.md`, and evidence/validation artifacts instead of legacy or ad hoc layouts.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#2-new-project-setup-does-not-enforce-the-current-work-folder-planning-structure`

## Scope
- Update setup templates, generated agent instructions, skills, and command prompts to use `docs/work/<date>-<slug>/`.
- Teach `plan.md` as the default work-item planning document.
- Teach `tasks.md`, `decisions.md`, and validation/evidence files as sibling artifacts.
- Teach the minimum evidence artifact fields: issue/work item id, stage/task id, command/run id, command string or tool name, exit code/verdict, timestamp, actor/session, stdout/stderr or log path, source file refs, commit SHA/revision/hash, redaction state, provenance/source type, and links back to task/decision entries.
- Keep `design.md` as a durable architecture/product design artifact or backward-compatible legacy input.
- Cover existing-project update/repair, not just greenfield setup: `forge update`, `forge setup --repair`, generated harness preview/apply flows, and stale instruction migration.
- Include concrete renderer/template surfaces: `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md` projections, `.claude/commands/**`, `.claude/rules/**`, cross-agent skills/templates, `docs/reference/AGENT_SKILL_PARITY.md`, `lib/harness-capability-matrix.js`, and packages/skills templates.
- Add fresh-project fixtures for all supported agent harnesses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A fresh `forge setup` project includes guidance for the preferred work-folder structure.
- An existing Forge project can preview and apply the updated artifact contract without surprise dirty generated files.
- `/plan`, `/dev`, `/validate`, `/ship`, `forge plan`, and generated skills agree on artifact names and locations.
- Fresh and existing-project guidance explains how validation evidence links to `tasks.md` and `decisions.md` so future `forge recap` can cite proof instead of loose notes.
- Tests prove new projects do not regress to legacy `design.md` as the default work-item plan.
- Documentation explains when `design.md` is still appropriate.
title: Align fresh Forge setup with docs/work plan task decision structure

description:
## Description
Define the gate before Forge allows multi-machine/team issue writes.

## Scope
- Team writes require server-side serialized project authority.
- Cloudflare Durable Object per project remains the planned primitive.
- D1/read models, queues, and projections are downstream of authority, not authority themselves.
- Local SQLite mode remains single-machine/many-worktree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs and future commands refuse unsupported multi-machine local-write assumptions.
- Server sequence/entity revision model is documented before implementation.
- Projection outbox/dead-letter behavior is tied to team authority plan.
title: Gate multi-machine team writes on serialized authority

acceptance_criteria: Model is sufficient before any multi-machine write implementation begins.

description:
## Description
The roadmap needs explicit release lanes and version-level sequencing so the team knows which core self-hosting issues must land before the next feature release and which work can follow later.

Reference: `docs/work/2026-06-06-kernel-backlog-memory-roadmap/workflow-friction-amendments.md#8-release-sequencing-is-not-explicit-enough`

## Scope
- Define near-term release lanes: self-hosting stability, new-project setup correctness, Kernel/TS state foundation, Knowledge/architecture capture, and team authority.
- Assign existing and new issues to probable release lanes without pretending exact dates are guaranteed.
- Mark dependencies so downstream features do not start before setup/state/hooks foundations are ready. This issue defines the ordering and should not be blocked by the broad parent lanes it is sequencing.
- Add release gates for clean push/verify/merge lifecycle, fresh setup correctness, worktree state, and Dolt hot-path reduction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `issue-map.md` includes a release-lane table with issue IDs and dependency notes.
- Next-release gates explicitly prioritize self-hosting workflow reliability and new-project adoption correctness.
- Implementation order is updated to avoid starting downstream Knowledge/hook UX features before core state/setup blockers.
- The release smoke gate includes clean checkout + linked worktree + generated harness + hooks + Forge state + projection state completing push/review/verify/merge/post-merge cleanup without stash/manual repair.
- Evaluator review confirms release sequencing has no critical missing dependency.
title: Define release lanes and gates for next Forge feature releases

description:
After reading actual Superpowers skill files (brainstorming, writing-plans, systematic-debugging), confirmed gaps vs our workflow:

1. YAGNI not enforced during /plan Phase 3 task writing — Superpowers brainstorming enforces 'ruthless YAGNI', writing-plans enforces 'DRY, YAGNI, TDD'. Forge has no YAGNI gate.
2. Task format missing complete code — Superpowers writing-plans requires exact file paths + line numbers + complete working code per task. Forge tasks describe work but don't include actual code.
3. No /debug command — Superpowers systematic-debugging is a full 4-phase skill with HARD-GATE: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST. Forge has nothing equivalent.
4. Alternatives gate missing in /plan Phase 1 — Superpowers brainstorming step 3 requires presenting 2-3 alternatives before design approval. Forge HARD-GATE doesn't require this.
5. DRAI — user mentioned this term but it doesn't appear in any Superpowers skill file. Needs clarification from user before acting.

Research source: actual GitHub files fetched from obra/superpowers (brainstorming/SKILL.md, writing-plans/SKILL.md, systematic-debugging/SKILL.md)
notes:
Design doc at docs/plans/2026-03-02-superpowers-gaps-design.md. Branch: feat/superpowers-gaps (to be created). 4 changes: DRY gate in plan Phase 2, YAGNI filter in plan Phase 3, verification HARD-GATE in dev, /check renamed to /validate with 4-phase debug mode.
Implementation complete: 6 tasks done (0a, 0b, 1, 2, 3, 4), 0 decision gates fired. Final: 1227 pass, 0 fail. Ready for /validate.
title: Superpowers workflow gaps: YAGNI enforcement, /debug command, planning quality

description:
## Problem
The project renamed /check → /validate and /merge → /premerge, but several files still use the old names, creating confusion for users and agents.

## Fixes Required
1. .cursorrules still documents /check and a flow that disagrees with AGENTS.md and docs/WORKFLOW.md
2. The hardcoded CURSOR_RULE string in bin/forge.js (shipped to Cursor users) still uses /check, /merge, and a mismatched step list
3. SKILL_CONTENT in bin/forge.js agrees with /validate + /premerge while CURSOR_RULE does not — internal contradiction in the same file
4. CLAUDE.md in the repo root still has a one-line placeholder instead of real project instructions
5. README, package.json description, and tier copy disagree on how many agents and which ones are 'supported'

description: createEvalWorktree() in scripts/lib/eval-runner.js creates git worktrees branched from HEAD with git worktree add -b. Tests inside eval worktrees run git init + git commit which leak 'initial commit' by author 'Test <test@test.com>' onto the eval branch. Since eval worktrees share the git object store with the parent repo, these commits become reachable from feature branches. This has corrupted 7+ PRs with 'initial commit' that deletes 139,885 lines. Fix: (1) Use --detach instead of -b in createEvalWorktree, (2) Add cleanup hook to destroyEvalWorktree that prunes unreachable objects, (3) Add a pre-push hook check that rejects commits by author 'Test <test@test.com>'.
title: Eval worktrees leak rogue commits onto feature branches — kills PRs

acceptance_criteria: stage transitions include summary+decisions+artifacts+next, validate subcommand warns on missing fields, AGENTS.md documents convention, all commands reference validate
description:
## Problem
AI agents and users often create beads issues, design docs, and stage transitions with minimal context. When revisiting later (different session, different agent, or after compaction), the lack of pretext makes it hard to understand what happened and why.

## What Needs to Change
1. **CLAUDE.md**: Add a rule that all beads issues, design docs, and planning artifacts must include enough context to be understood standalone — not just a title but the why, what, and how.
2. **Commands (plan/ship/review)**: Embed enforcement in each command — e.g., /plan must write detailed descriptions, /ship must explain PR narrative with Problem/Root Cause/Fix/Value, beads updates must include context.
3. **AGENTS.md**: Add as a core principle alongside Design-First and TDD-First — 'Context-Rich Documentation: every artifact must be self-explanatory to a future reader with no prior context.'

## Examples of What Good Looks Like
- Beads issue: Not just 'Fix ship command' but full Problem/Root Cause/Fix structure
- Design doc: Not just bullet points but explanations of why each decision was made
- Stage transitions: Not just 'plan → dev' but what was accomplished in the completed stage

## Scope
Convention enforcement across CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and command files (plan.md, ship.md, review.md, validate.md). No runtime code changes.
title: Enforce descriptive context convention across workflow (CLAUDE.md + commands + AGENTS.md)

description:
Typed memory API thin layer — convention + light tooling, NOT a new datastore. Per D22 (memory taxonomy) + D31 (lean on Beads): routes the 7 memory categories to existing backends and enforces categorization + provenance, but stores nothing itself.

Categories -> backends:
- decisions -> docs/plans/*.md (canonical), `bd remember decision:<slug>` (index)
- episodes -> `.beads/interactions.jsonl` via `bd audit record` (per D23)
- skills -> .claude/skills/ + .forge/extensions/local/ (per D20 ownership matrix)
- state -> .forge/state.json (Forge-owned)
- issues -> Beads (`bd create` / `bd update`)
- audit -> `bd audit record` (per D23, hash-chained)
- preferences -> `bd remember pref:<key>`

Layer enforces: every write supplies category + provenance (who/why/source); every read tags the category for budget accounting. ~2 days work, no new storage.

ACCEPTANCE
1. lib/memory/typed-api.js with 7 typed write functions, all routing to existing backends.
2. Provenance metadata mandatory on every write.
3. Migration script tags existing artifacts (docs/plans, .beads, .claude/skills) with categories.
4. Read API returns category-tagged results for budget telemetry.
notes: 2026-05-29 authority reset: partially superseded. Typed memory concept survives, but no-new-datastore and Beads-backed framing are replaced by Kernel-backed memory projection under forge-2agy.5.
title: Typed memory API thin layer (7 categories, no new datastore)

description:
Implement the canonical `/plan` skill using the iteration-driven 5-phase method demonstrated by this very planning session (locked-decisions.md D1–D38 across 6 iterations is the proof-of-concept).

Phases:
1. Intent capture — one-question-at-a-time Q&A to lock the design problem.
2. Parallel research — multiple research subagents fan out on the locked intent.
3. Parallel critics — anti-architect / gap-finder / sequencer critics review research output simultaneously.
4. Synthesis + iteration loop — collapse critic outputs into locked decisions; re-fan if convergence threshold not met; supersede earlier decisions inline.
5. Final thesis lock — emit FINAL-THESIS.md + locked-decisions.md with supersedes tracking.

Each phase is HARD-GATEd. User-configurable depth: skip phases, lock convergence threshold (e.g., "3 iterations max"), pick critic set.

Evidence base: docs/work/2026-04-28-skeleton-pivot/ (this folder) — 38 decisions, 6 iterations, supersedes-tracked, FINAL-THESIS.md as exemplar artifact.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Skill registered at .claude/skills/plan/ with description-match auto-invoke per D35.
2. 5 phases each with explicit gate condition and `forge options why` doc.
3. Reproduces a (smaller-scope) FINAL-THESIS + locked-decisions pair on a sample feature.
4. Convergence threshold + critic set configurable via .forge/config.yaml.
5. Skip-phase flags (`--skip-research`, `--lock-after=3`) work end-to-end.
title: Iteration-driven planning skill (/plan, 5 phases)

description:
Enhance /plan and /dev skills to automatically store plan data directly in Beads issue fields instead of relying solely on external design doc files.

**Problem:** When resuming across sessions, agents must read the Beads issue AND separately find/read the design doc. Progress state (e.g., 'task 3 of 7 done') is lost entirely between sessions.

**Solution:** Use Beads built-in fields:
- `--design`: Task breakdown/plan from /plan Phase 3
- `--acceptance`: Definition of done criteria
- `--append-notes`: Progress checkpoints as /dev completes each task
- `--description`: Keep linking to design doc, but embed key context inline

**Files to modify:**
- `.claude/commands/plan.md` — after Phase 3 task list, auto-run bd update with --design and --acceptance
- `.claude/commands/dev.md` — after each task completes, auto-run bd update --append-notes with progress

**Recovery flow after change:**
`bd show <id>` → design field has task list + notes field has progress = full context in one command
title: Beads-embedded plan context — auto-populate design/notes/acceptance from /plan and /dev

description:
﻿## Problem
Forge setup currently mixes three different concerns:
1. workflow asset shipping/runtime correctness,
2. invasive repository side effects during setup,
3. weak workflow enforcement and inconsistent install/docs UX.

The current PR should stay focused on shipping blockers that make the advertised agent workflow unusable. The remaining work needs a separate follow-up epic so it can be designed and shipped without expanding PR #111 into a product-policy rewrite.

## Scope For Follow-up PR
### Setup side effects
1. Make Beads opt-in instead of default during setup.
2. Stop auto-committing during setup unless explicitly requested.
3. Stop rewriting git hook configuration by default (including core.hooksPath).
4. Make --skip-external mean no repo-level side effects.
5. Improve setup summary to explicitly report files created, git config changed, hooks changed, commits made, and services initialized.

### Workflow enforcement
6. Replace instruction-only hard gates with real runtime-enforced checks where the workflow claims enforcement.
7. Fail early when required workflow scripts/helpers are missing instead of allowing partial /plan or /dev execution.
8. Reduce split-brain behavior between prompt instructions, Beads hooks, and shell helpers.

### Install / dependency management
9. Prefer documented durable install flows such as bun add -D forge-workflow over ad hoc bun install usage.
10. Make lefthook/install messaging consistent and deterministic.
11. Audit packaging/docs so installed behavior matches documented setup paths.

### Windows / IDE / docs UX
12. Improve Windows/Cursor guidance around Bash-centric flows.
13. Document hidden worktree behavior in IDEs when ignore patterns obscure .worktrees.
14. Make Codex support explicit about what is fully runnable vs partially scaffolded.
15. Make --help and setup docs quieter and more transparent about side effects.

## Out Of Scope For This Epic
- The immediate shipping/runtime blockers for PR #111, including missing scaffolded runtime assets for generated workflow commands.
- Narrow parity fixes that are required to make the current PR's generated command surface runnable.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Default forge setup does not initialize Beads, rewrite hooks, or auto-commit without explicit opt-in.
2. --skip-external performs a side-effect-minimized setup.
3. Missing workflow prerequisites fail loudly and immediately.
4. Docs recommend reproducible install paths and match actual runtime behavior.
5. Windows/Cursor/Codex workflow expectations are documented clearly.
notes:
Additional follow-up candidates from downstream validation on 2026-04-02: (1) align plan.md examples with real bd worktree CLI syntax; (2) add explicit Phase 1 Q&A gate with waiver flag/env and logged skip; (3) document worktree discoverability because bd adds .worktrees to .gitignore and IDEs hide it; (4) add Cursor-specific plan gate/rule/checklist for main -> bd worktree -> bd epic -> Phase 1 Q&A or waiver; (5) add AGENTS.md guidance that no design/task artifacts should be written before the hard gate unless the user explicitly opts out; (6) document downstream/local quick win: if a consumer repo vendors command templates without helper scripts, either scaffold minimal scripts or trim copied commands to match shipped assets.
Decomposed on 2026-04-02 into child issues for clearer execution: forge-dq8j.1 (setup defaults / side effects), forge-dq8j.2 (workflow enforcement and plan gating), forge-dq8j.3 (install/docs/help/IDE parity). Use this epic as the umbrella only; implement from the child issues.
title: Epic: Setup hardening — side effects, enforcement, and install UX

description:
﻿## Problem
Current setup is too invasive for users who only want workflow assets installed. It initializes Beads, rewrites hook configuration, and may create commits without clear opt-in. That makes setup harder to trust and harder to adopt in existing repositories.

## Scope
1. Make Beads initialization opt-in instead of part of default setup.
2. Remove automatic setup commits unless explicitly requested.
3. Stop rewriting git hook configuration by default, including core.hooksPath.
4. Make --skip-external perform a true side-effect-minimized setup.
5. Improve the setup summary so it clearly reports repo-level side effects.

## Why This Is Separate
This is a product-behavior and default-policy change. It should not be mixed into the current shipping-fix PR, which is focused on making generated workflow commands runnable after setup.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Default forge setup does not initialize Beads unless the user explicitly opts in.
2. Default forge setup does not make commits.
3. Default forge setup does not rewrite hook configuration.
4. --skip-external avoids repo-level integrations and side effects.
5. Setup output explicitly reports files created, git config changed, hooks changed, commits made, and services initialized.
title: Setup defaults — reduce side effects and require opt-in

description:
﻿## Problem
Forge describes hard-gated workflow stages, but several of those gates currently rely on prompt instructions or helper scripts that may not exist in consumer repos. That allows partial /plan or /dev execution without the promised runtime enforcement.

## Scope
1. Add a real Phase 1 Q&A gate for /plan.
2. Require an explicit waiver when Phase 1 is skipped, such as a flag or env var, and log that waiver.
3. Fail early when required workflow helpers are missing instead of continuing with partial behavior.
4. Reduce split-brain behavior between prompt instructions, shell helpers, and Beads-driven workflow checks.
5. Add AGENTS/docs guidance that design/task artifacts should not be written before the hard gate unless the user explicitly opts out.
6. Add Cursor/Codex-friendly plan gate guidance so the workflow is enforced consistently in IDE-driven sessions.

## Why This Is Separate
This work changes the workflow contract itself. It needs deliberate design and should not be bundled into the narrower shipping/runtime fixes in the current PR.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. /plan enforces either Phase 1 Q&A completion or an explicit logged waiver.
2. Missing required helpers fail loudly and immediately.
3. Workflow docs and runtime behavior agree on what is actually enforced.
4. Artifact creation is blocked before the hard gate unless explicitly waived.
5. IDE-focused guidance exists for plan gating and worktree/epic setup order.
title: Workflow enforcement — real plan gates and prerequisite failures

description:
﻿## Problem
Install guidance, packaged behavior, and IDE ergonomics are inconsistent. Users can end up with a partially durable install, noisy help output, hidden worktrees in IDEs, and unclear Codex/Cursor expectations.

## Scope
1. Align docs with durable install paths such as bun add -D forge-workflow.
2. Make install and lefthook messaging consistent and deterministic.
3. Audit packaging/docs so documented setup paths match shipped runtime behavior.
4. Align plan.md examples with the real bd worktree CLI syntax.
5. Document that .worktrees may be hidden in IDEs because they are gitignored, and explain how to open them.
6. Improve Windows/Cursor guidance for Bash-centric workflows.
7. Clarify Codex support so docs distinguish fully runnable workflow support from partial scaffolding.
8. Make --help and setup output quieter and more transparent.

## Why This Is Separate
This is mostly docs, UX, and packaging consistency work. It is important, but it should not dilute the current PR's shipping-blocker fixes.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Install docs recommend durable dependency installation.
2. Help/setup output is quiet and predictable.
3. plan.md examples match real bd commands.
4. Worktree discoverability is documented for IDE users.
5. Windows/Cursor/Codex guidance reflects actual supported behavior.
6. Packaging and docs no longer describe behavior that the product does not ship.
title: Install and UX parity — packaging, docs, help, and IDE discoverability

description:
## Problem
/ship pushes the branch and creates a PR without checking if the branch is up-to-date with master. If master advanced while the worktree was in /dev, the PR shows as 'out of date' and may have merge conflicts. The PR template also focuses on implementation details rather than the 'why' behind the change.

## Root Cause
Worktrees are isolated by design — bd worktree create branches from master at creation time. If master advances (other PRs merged), the worktree branch falls behind. Neither /validate nor /ship check for this before proceeding.

## Fix (3 changes, 2 files)
1. **validate.md**: Add rebase onto origin/master as first step in entry HARD-GATE. Abort+alert on conflicts. Ensures all validation checks run against code that includes latest master changes.
2. **ship.md**: Add lightweight freshness safety check (git fetch + behind check) before push. Catches cases where master advanced between /validate and /ship.
3. **ship.md**: Replace PR template — Problem/Root Cause/Fix/Value (visible) with test/security/design doc in collapsible details section.

## Affects
- .claude/commands/validate.md
- .claude/commands/ship.md
- All 7 agent directories (via sync-commands.js)

## Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-03-22-ship-rebase-design.md

## Follow-up

description:
**Problem:** When a worktree is created outside `forge worktree create` (T3 Code, Claude Code EnterWorktree, manual `git worktree add`), the Beads Dolt database is missing. `.beads/dolt/` is gitignored so it doesn't transfer. All `bd` commands fail with 'database forge not found.'

**Root cause:** `setupBeads()` in `lib/commands/worktree.js:118-149` creates a symlink from worktree `.beads` → main repo `.beads`, but only `forge worktree create` calls it. External tools don't.

**Solution:** Create `lib/beads-bootstrap.js` with auto-detection + 3-tier bootstrap:
1. Symlink to main repo `.beads` (preferred — shares live DB)
2. Copy backup from main repo + `bd backup restore` (fallback for Windows permission issues)
3. Fresh init with warning (last resort)

**Trigger points:** `forge status`, `bd prime`, `bd doctor --fix`

**Files:**
- `lib/beads-bootstrap.js` (new)
- `lib/commands/worktree.js` (extract setupBeads)
- `scripts/smart-status.sh`
- `test/commands/worktree.test.js`
title: Beads bootstrap for external worktrees — auto-detect and 3-tier recovery

description:
﻿## Problem
Forge currently relies on raw `.beads` state and direct `bd` usage as the practical collaboration path. That works for local and multi-session workflows, but it is a poor shared coordination surface for multiple developers and multiple agents.

The desired model is:
- GitHub Issues = shared team-visible issue state
- Forge = authority layer for workflow/state/sync rules
- Beads = local backend/cache behind Forge

Without that boundary, the project keeps paying dual-write drift costs: agents can mutate `bd` and `gh` separately, `.beads` needs to be pushed just to share state, and future MCP integration has no stable Forge-owned API to call.

## Scope
1. Make Forge the supported write path for issue operations.
2. Treat Beads as a backend/cache, not the developer-facing authority.
3. Treat GitHub Issues as the shared collaboration surface for issue identity/state.
4. Define how workflow state, handoff context, and project memory stay Forge-owned instead of being pushed entirely into GitHub.
5. Sequence the wrapper, sync, import, and memory work so each layer builds on a stable authority model.

## In Scope Issues
- forge-ya9l ? Forge CLI abstraction layer for issue commands
- forge-nlgg ? bidirectional Forge/Beads/GitHub sync
- forge-ij1 ? import existing GitHub issues into local Forge/Beads state
- forge-xdh7 ? agent-agnostic project memory

## Out Of Scope
- Replacing or forking Beads immediately
- Full GitHub field mirroring for every Beads field
- Making GitHub the source of truth for rich agent workflow context
- Building the MCP server before Forge owns the command boundary

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Agents can use Forge commands instead of raw `bd`/`gh` for normal issue work.
2. Shared cross-developer issue state no longer depends on pushing raw `.beads` state as the primary transport.
3. GitHub and local Forge/Beads state have a clear authority split and reconciliation model.
4. Shared memory/context work has a clear home in Forge rather than agent-specific stores.
notes:
2026-05-29 authority reset: superseded by the Forge Kernel plan. Local Forge Kernel is source of truth; Beads is import/export only; GitHub/Linear sync moves to server-side projection workers after Cloudflare team authority. Do not implement bidirectional local authority before 0.0.25 projection workers.
2026-05-29 final tracker alignment: this epic is non-actionable until the 0.0.25 Cloudflare Team Authority And Projection Workers release. Beads does not allow a feature issue to block an epic directly, so the epic is blocked by the Forge Kernel release-train epic and its actionable children are blocked behind 0.0.25.
title: Superseded: GitHub/Beads coordination projection archive

description: Users installing the sync plugin face different branch protection scenarios. Document each path with step-by-step instructions, screenshots, and a decision tree ('What branch protection do you have? → Follow path X'). Also consider: auto-detecting branch protection during forge setup and recommending the right path, simplifying PAT creation with a setup wizard or CLI command (gh auth), offering a 'no-push' mode that creates a PR instead of direct push for strict repos.

description:
﻿## Problem
Teams adopting Forge often already have active GitHub issues. They need a way to seed local Forge/Beads state from GitHub without making raw `.beads` history the onboarding mechanism.

## Scope
1. Add a Forge-driven pull/import path for existing GitHub issues.
2. Map imported GitHub issue identity, state, labels, assignee, and links into local Forge/Beads-backed issue state.
3. Create and maintain mapping entries so later sync operations reconcile against the same linked issues.
4. Align the import with the authority split: GitHub for shared issue identity/state, Forge for local workflow/context state, Beads as backend/cache.

## Out Of Scope
- Importing every GitHub comment or discussion into Beads
- Treating imported local state as the new source of truth over GitHub
- Full GitHub project-board migration tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Existing GitHub issues can be imported into local Forge/Beads state with stable mappings.
2. Imported issues participate in the same Forge-owned sync model as newly created issues.
3. Import does not require pushing raw `.beads` state to establish shared coordination.
notes: 2026-05-29 authority reset: superseded by the Forge Kernel plan. GitHub issue import should not lead before Kernel authority. Reframe this under 0.0.25 projection/import work after server authority exists.
title: Forge team sync --pull: import existing GitHub issues into Forge-backed local state

description:
## Problem
postinstall in package.json runs 'node ./bin/forge.js' which triggers minimalInstall(). This causes surprising file changes and console output on every 'npm install' of the package as a dependency. Users don't expect installing a package to write files into their project.

## Fixes Required
1. postinstall runs node ./bin/forge.js → minimalInstall() — surprising file changes and console output on every install

## Improvements
- Customer UX: Document prominently on npm README; or make minimal install opt-in
- Technical: Idempotent, quiet-by-default, or scoped to when INIT_CWD looks like a real app project

Forge setup should be intelligent about what files it creates, when, and for which agents. No noise, no empty templates, no files for agents the user doesn't use.

4. One clear summary instead of wall of output
5. Additive agent setup (add agents incrementally without touching existing config)

description:
﻿## Problem
Forge needs a machine-readable workflow state model that is authoritative for stage and status decisions instead of inferring behavior from prompts, helper scripts, or ad hoc heuristics.

## Scope
1. Define and persist structured Forge-owned workflow state.
2. Use that state for stage enforcement and status reporting.
3. Keep this layer separate from GitHub issue identity/state sync: GitHub covers shared team issue coordination, while Forge workflow state remains richer and local/project-owned.

## Out Of Scope
- GitHub issue synchronization itself
- Rich project memory storage
- Replacing Beads as the local backend

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Forge can read and write authoritative workflow state without relying on prompt-only conventions.
2. Status logic and enforcement consume the same underlying state model.
3. The boundary between workflow authority and GitHub shared issue state is explicit.
title: Structured workflow state and status authority

description:
Before a developer starts any work, automatically surface open Beads issues that might be a prerequisite or conflict with what they are about to change. Prevents devs from making changes unaware of preceding/blocking work.

Problem: Developer opens a file and makes a quick change without knowing another issue is in-progress that restructures that same area. Merge conflict, rework, or logic inconsistency results.

Implementation options (to decide in /plan):
1. /plan Phase 1 HARD-GATE — before brainstorming, run: bd list --status=open|in_progress, show issues touching same feature area, ask dev to confirm no conflicts
2. Pre-commit hook — on git add of specific files, check bd list for issues owning those files (stored in issue metadata)
3. /status enhancement — when resuming work, show 'heads up: these open issues touch overlapping areas'
4. Combination: lightweight /plan check + pre-commit warning

Core behavior:
- Given files/area being changed, query Beads for open issues in same domain
- If a DEPENDS-ON chain exists (e.g. forge-ctc depends on forge-uto), warn dev if they are starting forge-ctc work before forge-uto is closed
- Surface assignee info ('Dev X is working on forge-uto which modifies bin/forge.js — you are also modifying bin/forge.js')
- Non-blocking by default (warn, don't hard-stop) unless dep issue is open and unassigned

Deliverables:
- /plan Phase 1 step: Beads cross-check before any design work
- Optionally: pre-commit hook addition to lefthook.yml
- Documentation in AGENTS.md workflow section

description:
﻿## Problem
Cross-developer visibility currently depends too much on syncing raw `.beads` state. That makes local backend state do double duty as the team collaboration surface.

The desired model is:
- GitHub Issues = shared team state for identity, assignee, labels, open/closed status, and board visibility
- Forge = authority for sync rules and normalized reconciliation
- Beads = local execution backend/cache

## Scope
1. When Forge issue commands mutate local issue state, sync the normalized shared fields to linked GitHub issues.
2. When linked GitHub issues change, pull those shared fields back into local Forge/Beads state.
3. Maintain durable mapping between Forge/Beads issues and GitHub issues.
4. Keep GitHub Projects v2 integration optional and derived from labels/status rather than making Projects the authority.
5. Stop treating raw `.beads` pushes as the primary cross-developer synchronization mechanism.

## Out Of Scope
- Full field mirroring for every Beads field
- Storing rich workflow context or agent memory entirely in GitHub
- Replacing Beads as the local issue engine

## Acceptance Criteria
1. GitHub becomes the shared team-facing issue surface without requiring raw `.beads` pushes as the main sync path.
2. Forge owns reconciliation logic between GitHub and local Beads-backed state.
3. The sync contract is based on normalized Forge-owned semantics, not ad hoc direct `bd`/`gh` mutations.
4. Project/board integration remains additive, not the system of record.
notes: 2026-05-29 authority reset: superseded by the Forge Kernel plan. Bidirectional GitHub sync becomes server-side projection work, not local issue authority.
title: Bidirectional Forge-GitHub sync — normalized shared issue state

description: When /verify or manual cleanup tries to remove a worktree directory after PR merge, rm -rf fails with 'Device or resource busy' because Dolt server holds locks on .beads/dolt/ files. Each worktree gets its own Dolt process. Nothing stops the Dolt server when work is done. On Windows, file locks are mandatory (not advisory), making this particularly bad. Fix: forge worktree remove must stop Dolt before cleanup. Add forge clean utility for batch cleanup of merged worktrees.
notes: Repro: Create worktree, do work, merge PR, try rm -rf .worktrees/<name>. Fails on Windows with EBUSY. Workaround: taskkill //F //IM dolt.exe && sleep 1 && rm -rf
title: Worktree cleanup fails — Dolt server locks files after PR merge

description: Worktrees created by git worktree add or forge worktree create don't run bun install, so node_modules/.bin/lefthook is missing. This means raw git push from a worktree has zero pre-push hooks — no branch protection, no lint, no tests. This is a critical gap: the nonce token mechanism assumes lefthook is running to skip, but if lefthook itself is absent, there's nothing to skip or enforce. Fix: forge worktree create must run bun install (it does, but verify). Also consider installing lefthook globally or symlinking node_modules.
title: Lefthook missing in worktrees — no quality gates on raw git push

description:
Problem: /status currently shows bd ready (no blockers) and bd blocked separately, missing open issues that fall into neither category. It also doesn't rank issues intelligently by impact.

3. Highlight high-impact items (e.g. fixing forge-cpnj unblocks 3 downstream issues)
4. Group by recommended action: resume in-progress → unblock chains → new P2 work → backlog
5. Show dependency graph summary (what blocks what)
6. Consider project health signals (blocked ratio, stale issues, avg lead time)

Current gap: 24 active issues but status only surfaced 16, and without ranking by project impact.
title: Smarter /status command — rank all issues by priority, dependency impact, and project health

description:
﻿## Problem
Project memory currently lives in agent-specific stores, which breaks multi-agent workflows and makes context handoff depend on whichever agent wrote the last note.

## Scope
1. Define a Forge-owned project memory format that any supported agent can read.
2. Store decisions, preferences, policies, and reusable project context outside agent-specific private stores.
3. Keep this memory separate from GitHub shared issue state: GitHub is for team-visible issue coordination, not for all rich agent context.
4. Ensure the memory model can later be surfaced through Forge commands and MCP without coupling to a single agent vendor.

## Out Of Scope
- Making GitHub Issues the home for all project memory
- Continuing to rely on agent-specific hidden memory as the primary source
- Replacing issue tracking with memory storage

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Forge has a documented, agent-agnostic home for project memory.
2. Shared memory complements issue sync instead of overloading GitHub Issues.
3. The design is compatible with a future Forge MCP server.
title: Agent-agnostic Forge project memory — share context across agents

description:
﻿## Problem
Agents and humans can still mutate project work through raw `bd` and `gh` commands. That creates dual-write drift and prevents Forge from becoming the real authority layer for issue operations.

## Scope
1. Add Forge-native issue commands as the supported write path: `forge create`, `forge update`, `forge claim`, `forge close`, `forge show`, `forge list`, and `forge ready`.
2. Provide a canonical Forge-owned issue wrapper surface so future MCP/server integrations can call Forge rather than raw `bd`.
3. Keep Beads behind Forge as a backend/cache. Do not embed GitHub sync policy directly into these commands.
4. Normalize command handling, help text, and test coverage so issue operations are discoverable and reliable.

## Out Of Scope
- Replacing Beads
- Full GitHub sync logic
- Rich workflow-state/status authority beyond issue operations

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Normal issue lifecycle operations can be performed through Forge commands instead of raw `bd`.
2. The implementation uses safe subprocess invocation with no shell interpolation.
3. Tests cover happy-path command translation plus failure modes.
4. The new command surface is positioned as the foundation for GitHub sync and MCP integration.
notes:
Implemented Forge-backed issue wrappers: issue/create/update/claim/close/show/list/ready. Added registry command modules, targeted tests, and real Beads smoke checks on 2026-04-05.
PR prepared on branch codex/forge-issue-command-surface after fresh local validation on 2026-04-06.
title: Forge issue command surface — wrap bd commands under forge

2. Pre-push runs 'pkg test' while developers may only run 'bun test' locally — mental model split

## Improvements
- Technical: Pre-push should call the same script as npm run lint / bun run lint where possible
- UX: Clear error messages (already partly there); ensure Windows .cmd behavior documented
- Process: CONTRIBUTING states bypasses (LEFTHOOK=0, --no-verify) and when they're acceptable
